text: When working with data, a database helps you store and organize information efficiently. Two main types exist: SQL and NoSQL. SQL databases, like MySQL, use structured tables with rows and columns, ideal for complex queries that link related data. You write queries in SQL to retrieve specific details, such as customer names or sales totals. Data modeling in SQL involves planning table relationships—for example, linking orders to customers. To speed up performance, you can add indexes, which help find data faster without scanning every row. In contrast, NoSQL databases use flexible formats like documents or graphs, better for large, unstructured data. Choosing between them depends on your project’s needs for consistency or scalability.
